steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.sata: 'sata' is a participle forming a compound verb with 'est'
hints.aetas: 'aetas' is a noun — the subject of the sentence

hints.to eat: 'to eat' is 'edere/esse' — but 'est' here comes from 'esse' (to be)

steps.prompt: What tense and mood is 'sata est' together?

hints.Present passive indicative: Present passive would lack the participle 'sata' — 'sata est' is a perfect passive compound
hints.Pluperfect passive indicative: Pluperfect passive would use 'sata erat' — 'sata est' uses present 'est' for the perfect

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the principal parts of 'edere' (to eat)
hints.eo, ire, ivi, itum: These are the principal parts of 'ire' (to go)
hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the principal parts of 'ferre' (to carry)

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'aetas' with genitive 'aetatis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'aetas' is 3rd declension
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es with genitive -ei — 'aetas' has genitive 'aetatis'

hints.Present active participle: Present active participle would be 'serens' — 'sata' has the perfect passive -t- stem
hints.Gerundive: Gerundive would be 'serenda' — 'sata' is the 4th principal part stem of 'serere'
hints.Future active participle: Future active participle would be 'satura' — 'sata' is perfect passive

hints.Adverb modifying 'sata': The adverb 'first' would be 'primum' or 'primo' — 'prima' agrees with 'aetas' as an adjective
hints.Substantive noun (subject): 'prima' agrees with 'aetas' in gender and number — it modifies, not replaces, the subject
hints.Ablative of time: Ablative would be 'prima' only with a noun — here it's nominative agreeing with 'aetas'

translation: The golden age was established first
